concept: A surreal musical valley where stage relics become landscape and weather.
scene: A layered cut-paper collage shows a dreamlike mountain valley at dusk, built from stacked matte paper cliffs and misty blue distance, with a tall pale waterfall dropping into a river that widens into a circular reflective pool. Around that pool, simplified paper silhouettes of adult figures drift through a smoky forest edge, their forms softened by translucent red-orange vellum flames and haze. Crossing the sky and valley like a strange mobile bridge, the floating striped cylinders become giant cut-paper totems and fallen log-like beams, some suspended, some embedded in the landscape, each reduced to bold colour bands with crisp oval ends. On the near bank, a small round paper coracle holds an adult boatman poling across the water, while above him hangs the six-pointed badge as a star-shaped lantern or compass bloom. On a rocky ledge, a collage-like radio host figure in cap and headphones leans toward an old microphone, cut from tan and sepia paper scraps with waveform strips and poster fragments layered into his clothing and the ground. The circular pool doubles as a fisheye mirror, reflecting distorted studio rafters and a looming shoe shape at its rim, while a ring of fishnet-pattern paper legs becomes a theatrical grove of arching limbs and ballet shoes framing the water rather than surrounding a person. In the clouds and waterfall mist, two enormous blue ghost-portraits emerge as overlapping profile-like landforms, their faces suggested by torn cobalt paper edges, so the whole world feels like music, memory, stagecraft, and landscape fused into one flat-laid collage with deep cast shadows between layers.
prompt: Create one layered cut-paper collage - hard scissor-cut edges, occasional torn fringes, coloured card stock stacked with soft drop shadows between the layers. The image is a 16:9 music blog header. The circular pool doubles as a fisheye mirror, reflecting distorted studio rafters and a looming shoe shape at its rim, while a ring of fishnet-pattern paper legs becomes a theatrical grove of arching limbs and ballet shoes framing the water rather than surrounding a person. Weave in recognisable elements taken from the 8 uploaded album covers: source 1: floating striped cylinders; source 2: blurred adult silhouettes in smoky woodland light; source 3: small round boat with pole and heraldic star-badge; source 4: towering cliffside waterfall valley; source 5: adult radio host with vintage microphone and collage textures; source 6: circular fisheye mirror with looming shoe reflection; source 7: ring of fishnet-clad legs and dance shoes; source 8: overlapping blue double portrait faces. Transform the subjects, figures, creatures, objects, and symbols depicted in the uploaded covers so they stay recognisable, and compose them all onto one deliberate page unified by the paper texture, print treatment, and palette. Cut each album motif from coloured paper - simplified, hard-edged shapes layered over one another, every motif readable at a glance. Composition: Overlapping planes of paper building one scene together, depth coming from the shadows between layers. Colour direction: chalk white, cream, sepia tan, kraft brown, charcoal black - matte, chalky construction-paper colours drawn from the sleeves. Mood: mysterious, theatrical, nostalgic. Keep the scene safe and tasteful: feature only adults (no children, infants, or minors), and show no gore, wounds, body horror, medical or anatomical imagery, blank or milky eyes, nudity, or sexual content. Reinterpret any such source motif abstractly, and suggest rather than exactly replicate the likeness of any identifiable real person. Do not include any text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, or signage anywhere in the image. Avoid: readable text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, signage, grid layout, contact sheet, evenly tiled squares, raw album-cover thumbnails, photorealistic photograph, DSLR photo, 3D render, CGI, octane render, glossy 3D rendering, camera depth of field, lens bokeh, realistic skin texture, a posed group of people arranged for the camera, objects displayed on tables, trays, or plinths, one giant central sculpture as the centrepiece of the scene. Bold, confident, gallery-quality print design with a strong sense of place.
restylePrompt: Restyle this image as a layered cut-paper collage photographed flat, with real depth between the layers.
